name: Alto de las Poyatas Trail
name_original: Alto de las Poyatas desde Zarza-Capilla — ida y vuelta
description: Hike the easy 3.5-mile Alto de las Poyatas Trail, gaining 749 feet to the Cima de las Poyatas summit in under two hours.
region_intro: Hiking around Zarza-Capilla offers diverse landscapes in the Extremadura region of Spain, characterized by rugged mountains, rich valleys, and expansive steppes. The area is situated at the foot of the Sierra de las Poyatas and the Sierra del Torozo, providing varied terrain for hikers. Trails often traverse mountainous areas covered in pines, holm oaks, and scrubland, with some routes leading through olive groves and areas with crystalline water. The region also features historical sites and viewpoints, adding to the hiking experience. Best hiking trails around Zarza-Capilla The most popular hiking route is Peñón del Pez Trail, a 8.8 miles (14.2 km) trail that takes 4 hours 5 minutes to complete. This moderate route offers views of Capilla Castle and traverses Mediterranean landscapes. Another top favourite among local hikers is Sierra de Las Poyatas Circular Trail, a difficult 13.5 miles (21.7 km) path. This route features significant elevation gain and panoramic views from the summit of Las Poyatas. Local hikers also love the Cabeza del Bouy — circular by Ermita del Calvario and Sierra de la Vaca, a 9.1 miles (14.7 km) trail leading through varied terrain, often completed in about 4 hours 24 minutes. Hiking around Zarza-Capilla is defined by rugged mountains, rich valleys, and expansive steppes. The network offers options for easy, moderate, and difficult routes, catering to various ability levels. The routes in Zarza-Capilla are highly rated by the komoot community with an average score of 4.7 stars from 10 reviews. More than 20 hikers have used komoot to explore Zarza-Capilla's varied terrain.
region_faq:
Q: How many hiking trails are available in Zarza-Capilla?
A: Zarza-Capilla offers a selection of 14 hiking routes, catering to various preferences and skill levels. These include 5 easy, 5 moderate, and 4 difficult trails, ensuring there's an option for every hiker.

Q: What kind of terrain can I expect on hikes around Zarza-Capilla?
A: The terrain around Zarza-Capilla is diverse, ranging from rugged mountains to rich valleys and expansive steppes. Trails often traverse mountainous areas covered in pines, holm oaks, and scrubland. You might also encounter routes leading through olive groves and areas with crystalline water, particularly on paths like the one to Vercialejo.

Q: Are there any family-friendly hiking options in Zarza-Capilla?
A: Yes, Zarza-Capilla offers 5 easy hiking routes that are generally suitable for families. These trails provide a gentler introduction to the region's natural beauty without significant elevation challenges.

Q: Can I bring my dog on the hiking trails in Zarza-Capilla?
A: While the guide does not specify official dog policies for all trails, many natural areas in Extremadura are generally dog-friendly, especially if dogs are kept on a leash. It's always recommended to check local signage or specific trail regulations before heading out with your pet.

Q: What are some notable landmarks or viewpoints I can see while hiking?
A: Hikers can discover several interesting landmarks and viewpoints. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/3687196" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Castillo de Capilla&lt;/a&gt;, though in ruins, is a significant historical site visible from trails like the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294175776/penon-del-pez-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez Trail&lt;/a&gt;. For panoramic views, consider visiting the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5325739" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Ermita del Calvario&lt;/a&gt; or the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/highlight/5461647" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cima de las Poyatas (856 m)&lt;/a&gt;, which offers distinct vistas of the Valle de los Pedroches and Estepa de la Serena.

Q: Are there any circular hiking routes available?
A: Yes, Zarza-Capilla features several circular routes. A notable one is the difficult &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294250828/sierra-de-las-poyatas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Las Poyatas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which covers 21.7 km and offers significant elevation gain and panoramic views. Another option is the &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1293785574/cabeza-del-bouy-circular-by-ermita-del-calvario-and-sierra-de-la-vaca" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Cabeza del Bouy — circular by Ermita del Calvario and Sierra de la Vaca&lt;/a&gt;, a moderate 14.7 km route.

Q: What is the best time of year to go hiking in Zarza-Capilla?
A: The region's diverse landscapes make it suitable for hiking across multiple seasons. Sunsets from Las Poyatas are particularly noted for their beauty in summer. However, for comfortable hiking, spring and autumn generally offer milder temperatures, while winter can provide a different, often quieter, experience. Always check local weather conditions before your trip.

Q: What do other hikers say about the trails in Zarza-Capilla?
A: The hiking routes in Zarza-Capilla are highly rated by the komoot community, with an average score of 4.7 stars from 10 reviews. Hikers often praise the varied terrain, the stunning panoramic views from summits like Las Poyatas, and the blend of natural beauty with historical sites.

Q: Are there any historical or archaeological sites to explore on the trails?
A: Yes, the area is rich in history. Evidence of pre-Roman and Roman settlements can be found, particularly at Las Poyatas. Hikers can also explore the Cueva de Vercialejo and Pinturas Rupestres de Puerto Alonso, which contain ancient schematic cave paintings, offering a glimpse into the region's past.

Q: Is there parking available near the trailheads?
A: While specific parking details for every trailhead are not provided, Zarza-Capilla is a rural area, and parking is generally available in or near the village and at common starting points for popular trails. It's advisable to plan your route and check for designated parking areas beforehand.

Q: Are there any challenging hikes for experienced trekkers?
A: Yes, Zarza-Capilla offers 4 difficult routes for experienced hikers. The &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294250828/sierra-de-las-poyatas-circular-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Sierra de Las Poyatas Circular Trail&lt;/a&gt;, at 21.7 km with significant elevation gain, is a prime example. Another challenging option is the 17.1 km &lt;a href="https://www.komoot.com/smarttour/e1294241881/penon-del-pez-trail" target="_blank" rel="noopener"&gt;Peñón del Pez Trail&lt;/a&gt;, which also features substantial elevation changes.

Q: What kind of wildlife might I encounter on the trails?
A: The mountainous areas and scrubland around Zarza-Capilla are home to various wildlife. Hikers might spot animals such as deer, wild boars, and rabbits, particularly in quieter sections of trails that traverse pine and holm oak forests.
tips: Zarza-Capilla is a small town in the La Serena valley. One of the most interesting monuments in the town is the 16th century Church of Saint Bartholomew.

Beautiful views from the Cima de las Poyatas, located at 856 meters above sea level in the Sierra del Torozo. During our climb to the top, we may have to push the bike because of its inclination and difficulty.

faqs.question: How long does it typically take to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: The Alto de las Poyatas Trail is an easy hike that generally takes around 1 hour and 46 minutes to complete. This duration can vary based on your pace and how much time you spend enjoying the sights.

faqs.question: What is the difficulty level of the Alto de las Poyatas Trail?
faqs.answer: This trail is rated as easy, making it suitable for most hikers, including families and those looking for a relaxed outdoor experience. The elevation gain is moderate, with about 228 meters up.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de las Poyatas Trail a loop or an out-and-back route?
faqs.answer: The Alto de las Poyatas Trail is designed as a round trip, meaning it's a loop trail. You'll start and end in the same location, offering varied scenery throughout your hike.

faqs.question: Where does the Alto de las Poyatas Trail start, and is there parking available?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Zarza-Capilla. While specific parking details aren't available, it's common for trails starting in villages to have roadside parking or small public parking areas nearby. We recommend checking local signage upon arrival.

faqs.question: What can I see along the route?
faqs.answer: Along the trail, you'll encounter a couple of notable highlights. These include the historic Church of San Bartolomé of Zarza-Capilla and the Cima de las Poyatas (856 m), which offers a summit experience.

faqs.question: Is the Alto de las Poyatas Trail dog-friendly?
faqs.answer: While specific regulations for this trail are not available, many easy hiking trails in the region are dog-friendly. It's always best to keep your dog on a leash and ensure you clean up after them. Please check local signage for any specific restrictions.

faqs.question: Do I need a permit or pay an entrance fee to hike this trail?
faqs.answer: Based on available information, there are no specific permits or entrance fees required to hike the Alto de las Poyatas Trail. It appears to be freely accessible to the public.

faqs.question: What is the best time of year to visit the Alto de las Poyatas Trail?
faqs.answer: Given its location, spring and autumn generally offer the most pleasant hiking conditions with mild temperatures. Summer can be hot, while winter might bring cooler weather. Always check the local weather forecast before you go.

faqs.question: What kind of terrain can I expect on the trail?
faqs.answer: As an easy-rated hike, you can expect generally well-maintained paths. The terrain is likely to be a mix of dirt paths and possibly some rocky sections, typical for a rural hike with moderate elevation changes.

faqs.question: Are there any facilities like cafes or pubs near the trailhead?
faqs.answer: The trail starts from Zarza-Capilla, a village that likely offers basic amenities such as cafes or small shops. It's a good idea to bring your own water and snacks, especially if you plan to hike during off-peak hours.

faqs.question: What should I pack for a hike on the Alto de las Poyatas Trail?
faqs.answer: For this easy, relatively short hike, we recommend bringing comfortable walking shoes, water, snacks, sun protection (hat, sunscreen), and a light jacket depending on the weather. A camera is also great for capturing the views from the summit.

waypoints.name: Church of San Bartolomé of Zarza-Capilla
